verses.text: David asks God to bow down and hear, for he is poor and needy, godly yet wholly dependent, crying for mercy all the day. He pleads his need, not his worth. The lowly heart finds ready welcome at the throne of grace.

David asks God to bow down and hear, for he is poor and needy, godly yet wholly dependent, crying for mercy all the day. He pleads his need, not his worth. The lowly heart finds ready welcome at the throne of grace.

David asks God to bow down and hear, for he is poor and needy, godly yet wholly dependent, crying for mercy all the day. He pleads his need, not his worth. The lowly heart finds ready welcome at the throne of grace.
He asks God to gladden his soul, for 'thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive,' rich in mercy to all who call. God's nature is the ground of hope. He is more ready to pardon than we are to ask.
He asks God to gladden his soul, for 'thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive,' rich in mercy to all who call. God's nature is the ground of hope. He is more ready to pardon than we are to ask.
In the day of trouble he will call, sure that God will answer. Trial drives him to prayer. The believer's instinct in distress is to cry to the God who hears.
In the day of trouble he will call, sure that God will answer. Trial drives him to prayer. The believer's instinct in distress is to cry to the God who hears.
None among the gods is like the LORD; all nations He made shall worship Him, 'for thou art great... thou art God alone.' His uniqueness is supreme. The one true God will yet be owned by every people.
None among the gods is like the LORD; all nations He made shall worship Him, 'for thou art great... thou art God alone.' His uniqueness is supreme. The one true God will yet be owned by every people.
None among the gods is like the LORD; all nations He made shall worship Him, 'for thou art great... thou art God alone.' His uniqueness is supreme. The one true God will yet be owned by every people.

'Teach me thy way, O LORD... unite my heart to fear thy name,' he prays, praising God's great mercy that delivered his soul. He longs for a single-minded heart. Mercy received makes us seek to serve God undividedly.

'Teach me thy way, O LORD... unite my heart to fear thy name,' he prays, praising God's great mercy that delivered his soul. He longs for a single-minded heart. Mercy received makes us seek to serve God undividedly.

'Teach me thy way, O LORD... unite my heart to fear thy name,' he prays, praising God's great mercy that delivered his soul. He longs for a single-minded heart. Mercy received makes us seek to serve God undividedly.

Proud and violent men rise against him, but God is 'full of compassion, and gracious, longsuffering'; so he asks for 'a token for good.' He rests on God's character. Against cruel foes, the believer leans on the kindness of God.
